subject: Ecology
title: Stepping Stone Islands
description: A short lesson to help you apply Stepping Stone Islands.

hook.text: A chain of islands acts like stepping stones, letting species hop across vast ocean gaps.

buildup.text: Each island reduces the effective distance to the next, making colonization more likely.

discovery.text: The Aleutian Islands chain allowed species to move between Asia and North America.

twist.text: Losing one island in the chain can break the corridor and isolate populations permanently.

climax.text: Stepping stones explain why some archipelagos share species while isolated islands do not.

punchline.text: Islands in a row become a highway.

quiz.question: How do stepping stone islands help dispersal?
options: They reduce effective distance between landmasses
